summary: Single-patch deep-attack EXP-329 (review_class=deep-attack, target data/wins.json ["057"].detail_verdict_text) closes ISS-1879 (WIN-057.c3 stronger_arguments[2], defense_survives=3). Adds a ~1450-char paragraph-internal AIC/BIC defender-pivot rebuttal between the Clarke 1866/Bessel 1841 historical-baseline sentence and the closing in-sample-retrodiction sentence. Three-pronged response: (1) AICc small-sample correction at n=6/k=4 contributes 40 penalty units — arithmetic tell that n/k is below the regime where information-criterion selection is reliable, (2) AIC/BIC penalty calibrations presuppose application to new in-distribution samples, which OPEN-015's ±12° exclusion stipulation violates, (3) most fundamentally, information criteria adjudicate among candidates and cannot rescue any candidate from being absolutely worse than a non-nested competitor — WGS84's 0.01% RMSE / V13's 6.2% is a 600× gap no information-criterion argument bridges. Kernel of truth acknowledged: AIC/BIC are legitimate, standard nested-model-selection apparatus. Verdict (Misleading) unchanged. After patch: detail_verdict_text grows 1598→2908 chars. review_class=deep-attack (singleton curmudgeon review) — escalated by analyst-baby from null reclassification, upheld here because the AIC/BIC argument is genuinely new to this WIN, not refinement of existing prose.
